title: Seseli talassicum
taxonomy: Seseli talassicum is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and relationships with other species within the same family. The genus Seseli is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region, and Seseli talassicum is one of its species. The plant is further categorized as a perennial herb, indicating its growth habit and life cycle. Seseli talassicum is also classified as a wild carrot relative, showing its evolutionary connection to the domesticated carrot.

intro: Seseli talassicum, also known as Talasski's Seseli, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Seseli talassicum include seselin, apiol, and other essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Seseli talassicum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
